text: Hydrodissection is a technique wherein pressurized fluid is delivered into the tissue, entering into the plane of least resistance.
When performed from a midline instrument port, this technique allows the surgeon to overcome some of the challenges encountered from a midline instrument port.
The leaves of the broad ligament are divided using traction generated by suction.
Once a space is opened, fluid is injected into the tissue and dissection into the avascular plane is signified with ballooning of the distal tissue.
An incision is made with monopolar energy followed by transection of the bladder pillars.
